headline: DRYDEN GOLD Hits 252 g/t Au Over 0.5m — Three New High-Grade Zones Found at Gold Rock, Ontario
snippet: Dryden Gold (TSX-V: DRY) drilled 4.25m grading 32.87 g/t Au including 0.5m at 252 g/t Au at its Gold Rock target in Ontario. The Big Master gold system now extends across 600 metres width and remains open at depth, with a CA$11M, 32,000m drill program underway in 2026.

headline: ZIJIN MINING Wins Shareholder OK for C$5.5B Allied Gold Buyout — Three African Open-Pit Mines, Closing Late April
snippet: Allied Gold shareholders approved Zijin's C$44/share all-cash offer — a 27% premium — adding three large African open-pit mines producing ~400,000 oz Au/yr. The deal, Zijin's largest acquisition ever, is expected to close late April 2026 and vaults them to sixth-largest global gold producer.

headline: SILVER BUYING BLITZ: China Imports 790+ Tonnes in Two Months — Eight-Year Record Driven by Solar and Investment Demand
snippet: China imported over 790 tonnes of silver in January-February 2026 — the highest two-month total in eight years — as solar manufacturers front-ran the April 1 VAT rebate removal and retail investors piled into bars. Silver is up 134% year-over-year, with an all-time high of $93.77/oz struck on January 14.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver Hits 4,896 g/t Ag Over 1.3m — Launches Record 64,000m Drilling Campaign at Galena Complex
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) discovered 10 new high-grade veins at Idaho's Galena Complex — headlined by 4,896 g/t Ag and 3.95% Cu over 1.3m — and announced its largest-ever 64,000m drill program for 2026, including the first systematic campaign at the newly acquired Crescent Mine in decades.

headline: Core Lithium Greenlights Finniss Restart With A$290M Funding Package — First Spodumene Shipment Targeted Q4 2026
snippet: Core Lithium (ASX: CXO) approved the Finniss FID backed by A$290M from Glencore, InfraVia, and Nebari. Mining resumes at Grants Open Pit in May 2026, with first SC6 spodumene concentrate delivery in Q4 2026. Project NPV is A$1.1B at a long-term spodumene price of US$1,500/t.

headline: COPPER Set COMEX All-Time Record $6.61/lb in January — Goldman Expects 25%+ US Tariff Decision by Mid-Year
snippet: Copper hit a COMEX all-time high of $6.61/lb on January 29 as tariff front-running and green-transition demand converged. Goldman Sachs' base case calls for a 25%+ US refined copper tariff by mid-2026, while LME inventories have climbed to eight-year highs — a split market sending mixed signals.

headline: DOE Awards $2.7B to Restore American Uranium Enrichment — Trump's Section 232 Makes U3O8 a National Security Asset
snippet: The DOE committed $2.7B over ten years to rebuild domestic LEU and HALEU enrichment capacity. President Trump's January 14 Section 232 proclamation designated uranium a national security commodity, paving the way for import curbs on Russian and Kazakh supply and potential price floors for US producers.

headline: Coeur Mining Closes New Gold Acquisition — Seven-Mine North American Powerhouse Eyes $3B EBITDA in 2026
snippet: Coeur Mining (NYSE: CDE) completed its all-share acquisition of New Gold on March 20, adding two Canadian mines to create a seven-asset North American platform. The combined company targets $3B EBITDA and $2B free cash flow in 2026 from ~900,000 oz Au and 20M oz Ag production.

headline: Gold Holds Above $4,762/oz Heading Into Weekend — On Track For Third Straight Weekly Gain
snippet: Gold steadied above $4,700/oz as a softer US dollar and easing inflation fears post-US-Iran ceasefire provided support. The metal is up 46.8% year-over-year. Silver rallied more than 4% on the week to $75.68/oz; copper also on course for its third consecutive weekly advance.

headline: Mining M&A Value Hits 13-Year High at $93.7B in 2025 — Billion-Dollar Mega-Deals Up 68% From Prior Year
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $93.7B in 2025 — the highest since 2012's $129.3B — with deals over $1B rising 68% year-on-year. Gold was the most targeted commodity as majors used elevated valuations for bolt-ons. US and Canadian projects together attracted 66% of total capital deployed.

headline: Gold Up 47% But GDX Up Only 35% — Analysts See Deep Value Catch-Up Trade Forming At Start of Q2
snippet: Gold equities have dramatically underperformed the metal — the GDX gained ~35-40% versus gold's 47%+ move over the past year. Producers are now trading at 6.4x 2026E EV/EBITDA, a 10% discount to historical averages, and at a 44% P/NAV discount at spot prices — a setup analysts increasingly call compelling.

headline: GOLD SWOT: Silver Demand Unrelenting — China's 790-Tonne Import Surge and Producer P/NAV Discounts Dissected
snippet: Frank Holmes' weekly SWOT analysis highlights China's eight-year record silver import surge and notes gold producers now trade at a 44% P/NAV discount to spot prices versus their historical average. The analysis flags Hochschild Mining's 2026 production guidance of up to 328,000 AuEq oz as a standout beat.

headline: WHY 2026 Could Be the Year Uranium Finally Breaks Out — Under-Contracting Meets US Policy Tailwind
snippet: Utilities are 34 million lbs/yr under-contracted versus replacement-rate demand, a structural deficit building toward the 2030s. With uranium now a US national security asset and $2.7B in enrichment investment committed, analysts argue 2026 is the policy inflection year that could rerate the sector.

headline: Just How Much Gold Is There? — And Why Physical Scarcity Still Matters At $4,700/oz
snippet: Mine production hit a record 3,672 tonnes in 2025 — yet all the gold ever mined fits in fewer than four Olympic swimming pools. Money Metals examines why the metal's physical scarcity remains the foundational investment thesis, even at record nominal prices above $4,700/oz.
